rationale: EV-001 shows reg.exe (running at High integrity as corp\Administrator) explicitly adding the UserInitMprLogonScript registry value under HKCU\Environment, a well-known persistence mechanism that executes the specified payload at every user logon. A0 confirms the value was successfully written, pointing to C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe as the logon script — calc.exe is a classic stand-in/placeholder used during adversary testing or staging, strongly suggesting deliberate abuse rather than an accidental misconfiguration. The parent process is a bare cmd.exe with no visible command line context, which is consistent with interactive attacker activity or a dropped shell. No legitimate administrative tooling or software deployment context is present to explain this action, and the combination of a known persistence key, a suspicious payload placeholder, and a high-integrity interactive session makes benign explanations unlikely.
